blocks.content: The silence that seemed endless suddenly turned into a noisy murmur.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What, what's going on? Why is Aegis here?
In High Gear, it was very rare for a user to actually bow like that.
Even if threatened by a ranker, users would rather clean their codename than sell their pride.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Who's Yahweh2? Have you heard of him?

blocks.speaker: Aegis
blocks.content: No, it's my first time. And his parts are a mess. A basic Maron package with a bolt gun…
As people were stunned by the appearance of someone who looked like they were selling scrap metal, Shirone asked,

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I thought he would come himself.
He had guessed Aegis's identity when she referred to Fermi as 'boss.'

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So, it's the real Golden Wheel.'
Aegis bowed again.

blocks.speaker: Aegis
blocks.content: I'm sorry. The boss thought it would be better to handle Yahweh2's issue quietly.
Shirone understood.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Alright. Let's go now.
Aegis turned military-style and walked towards the northern exit of the square.
Shirone didn't mind the stares, but Destroyer707 and his group felt differently.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: This is the first time I've gotten this much attention. But who is Yahweh2, really?

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: They had thought he wasn't an ordinary user from the start, but that was within High Gear.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: What's happening? Who is this boss? Are we getting involved in something huge beyond our virtual world?

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Is it okay to go along with this?
Destroyer707 turned to see the same concern on his friends' faces.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ugh, oh…
When they came to their senses, they were in an alley in the northern slums of the city.
Junkies, high on drugs, were rolling on the ground, making strange noises.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Who are they?

blocks.speaker: Aegis
blocks.content: They're addicted to drug programs. High Gear is primarily a battle simulation, but being in the undercoder, its applications are vast. Also, being a floor coder makes it hard to track. The boss conducted clinical trials here too.
Angel's Tears, it was called.
Yaaah!
A bald couple slumped against a wall stuck out their tongues at Shirone, making strange noises.
Electricity sparked from their seven mechanical eyes.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Here, drug reactions can be quantified. It's more effective than biological experiments.
Shirone could guess why Fermi chose High Gear.
Soon, they saw a shabby motel with half-lit neon signs, and Aegis went straight to room 307.

blocks.speaker: Aegis
blocks.content: Boss, I've brought them.
Entering the room, they saw a man with a chilling appearance sitting on the bed.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Fermi?
User codename, Yolga's Son.
He had half-shaved red hair and tattoos that made his face look like a monster with black dye.
His eyes were white without pupils, and he wore a leather fur coat that devil worshippers might wear.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: …What is he, a villain boss?
In reality, Fermi played the role of a neat businessman, but his inner self seemed different.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Is that really Yolga's Son?
Unlike the bewildered Shirone, Destroyer707 and his group couldn't believe their eyes.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Awesome. A flawless ATX eye from Genosa. And it's matte.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: The appearance alone was intimidating, but the lack of pupils was excellent for close combat.
Fermi gestured to the table.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Sit down. By the way… you have company?
Forgetting it was a virtual world, Destroyer707 was at a loss.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Uh, hello. We are…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: They're my friends.
Fermi nodded as if that was enough and offered them seats too.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Nice to meet you. Have a seat.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Uh, okay.
Even sitting far from the bed, Destroyer707 and his group were dazed.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: What's going on?

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Codenames can be cleaned. So, meeting new users from large guilds required caution.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Can we not ask anything?

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Even without a clear definition, it felt like the balance of power was in Yahweh2's favor.
Fermi started the conversation.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: So, then…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hey! You!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You didn't tell me anything! The Tenmen, the Electronic Wasteland, it's huge! You did it on purpose, didn't you?

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Hey, why get mad over that? Honestly, it wasn't a big deal, right?
Looking back, it wasn't.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You have to go through that to be called Yahweh. Listen, I wasn't just playing around. About defeating the Operator, you haven't changed your mind, right?
Destroyer707 and his group exchanged glances.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: He's really doing it?

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: They had heard it, but Yahweh2 knew nothing about High Gear.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Now the leader of Golden Wheel is speaking.
The weight was entirely different, and they couldn't even imagine how it was possible.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I have no choice. I have to beat the Operator to get things done.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Just checking. Now that you understand the system, you know how difficult it is, right?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: …Yeah.
Shirone couldn't deny it.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Then this will be easier. That's why I didn't tell you. Your insight is much faster than my explanations.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Have you heard of the Ascension Guild?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: It's the top-ranked guild in the West Country, led by the Operator.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: They have many rankers and famous people like me.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: They rarely engage in public activities.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: They hunt at the highest difficulty levels inaccessible to regular users.
Fermi pointed west.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: She's difficult, probably a woman.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: She won't come to meet you.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: So, you'll have to find her in the hunting grounds.
Especially the first team of the Ascension Guild, which you can't break through.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: They operate command centers for guild tactics and strategies.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: It's impossible to carry out a personal mission in a battle where one mistake can lose the sun.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: I don't think it's a good idea.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I can do it.
Shirone didn't give up.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Solving problems is your job.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You have a plan, right? What should I do?
Fermi sighed and looked up.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: We give up the Solar War.
Destroyer707 and his group's expressions hardened.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: But that's betraying the guilds of East Country.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: The guild will collapse, even if we leave the Solar War.
Fermi didn't budge, and neither did Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: What's the probability?

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: 100 percent. Or zero percent.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: We'll keep marking the first team of Ascension with our bodies.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Even if we lose everything, we'll get you into the Solar Palace.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: That's my responsibility.
Destroyer707's mouth opened and closed.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: But, why go that far?
He glanced at Aegis, who also had a determined expression.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: How strong do I need to be?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Even if the probability is low, how strong do I need to be to make it possible?

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Hmm. Have you noticed anything strange since you got here?

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Like your senses being a bit different?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I still have my intuition.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's not the same as reality, but the signal system is identical.
Heh, I see.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: At least level 300.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Higher is better, but it's hard to reach that before the Solar War.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: You don't want to take more time either.
In reality, every day was critical.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: From level 300, the experience required to level up is absurd.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: So 300 is the best, and the rest depends on parts.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: But parts are also affected by level.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: This bolt gun is already too much for my output.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Not with prototypes.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: You need special legendary or artisan parts to complement energy efficiency.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: No matter what you do, your win rate won't exceed 5 percent.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: It's about how close you can get.
Fermi stood up.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Similarly, there's a limit to what I can give you now.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: I'll leave you with your friends until you reach level 100.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Get there in two days.
Destroyer707's eyes widened.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: What? Reach level 100 in two days?

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Equip the best parts and hunt high-level creatures.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Strong creatures aren't always the answer, but you know this phase better than I do.
Of course, they did.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: But if we do that, we'll have to keep buying new parts as we level up.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: What about the existing parts?

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Discard them.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: I'll send you a credit card approved by Golden Wheel.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Buy everything you need.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Use unlimited galaxies to get Yahweh2 to level 100 in two days.
Can you do it?
